description: Tests whether the SN Refsdal SX blind under-prediction vanishes in the high-precision (sigma_model -> 0) limit. A positive intercept disfavours the model-immaturity (GR) explanation.
verdict: IMMATURITY NOT EXCLUDED: the intercept is consistent with (or below) zero, so a high-precision GR model could plausibly remove the residual.
caveats: Small N (7 blind / 8 total) and shared lens inputs limit the regression.
The intercept is an extrapolation beyond the data's precision range.
Residual and sigma_model may be mildly correlated across method families.

Grillo+2024 is a post-blind precision update; it still retains a ~+14 d residual, which is the single strongest data point against the immaturity explanation, but post-blind models are not independent confirmations.

Schuldt et al. 2026 reach sub-percent statistical precision on dt_SX:S1 but are post-observation (use SX), so they cannot serve as a blind high-precision anchor; they bound what precision is achievable, not the blind residual.
headline: Precision-persistence (blind 7): sigma->0 residual intercept r0 = -1.2 +/- 24.6 d (one-sided p=0.519). Most-precise blind model (Oguri-a*, sigma=19 d) retains a +22 d residual.
